args.query: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN Close>Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close<Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close=Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days, COUNT(*) as total_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','IXIC','NYA') AND Date BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31' GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN Close>Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close<Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close=Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days, COUNT(*) as total_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','IXIC','NYA') AND Date BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31' GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"Indices in North America with more up days than down days in 2018:

- GSPTSE (Toronto Stock Exchange): 44 up days vs 41 down days
- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days

(Note: NYA had more down days: 36 up vs 42 down.)"

args.answer:
Indices in North America with more up days than down days in 2018:

- GSPTSE (Toronto Stock Exchange): 44 up days vs 41 down days
- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days

(Note: NYA had more down days: 36 up vs 42 down.)
